name: Tabernas Desert - Olula de Castro Loop
name_original: The Spaghetti Western of Spain — circular desde Tabernas
description: Challenging 40-mile road cycling route through the Tabernas Desert, famous for Spaghetti Western films, with 4255 feet of climbing.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Tabernas traverse Europe's only desert, characterized by arid plains, rocky slopes, and unique geological formations including canyons and badlands. The region is bordered by the Sierra de Los Filabres and Sierra de Alhamilla mountain ranges, providing varied topography for cyclists. This diverse landscape offers a mix of desert routes and challenging mountain ascents. The roads are generally well-maintained, providing a suitable environment for road cycling. Best road cycling routes around Tabernas The most popular road cycling route is The Spaghetti Western of Spain — circular desde Tabernas, a 40.0 miles (64.4 km) trail that takes 3 hours 42 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers a unique cycling experience through the desert landscape. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Climb to Colativi from Tabernas — circular route through the Sierra de Alhamilla, a difficult 43.2 miles (69.5 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain as it winds through the Sierra de Alhamilla. Local road cyclists also love the Climb to Alto de Velefique – Velefique climb loop from Complejo Oro Verde, an 81.1 miles (130.5 km) trail leading through the Sierra de Los Filabres, often completed in about 7 hours 31 minutes. Road cycling around Tabernas is defined by its unique desert scenery, challenging mountain climbs, and varied badlands. The network offers options for different ability levels, though many routes feature significant elevation. The routes in Tabernas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 1900 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Tabernas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Tabernas?
A: Tabernas offers a favorable climate for road cycling year-round, especially during the cooler months when other parts of Europe are colder. The region's semi-arid desert climate means warm temperatures are common, making autumn, winter, and spring ideal for avoiding the intense summer heat.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Tabernas?
A: The terrain around Tabernas is incredibly varied. You'll find routes traversing Europe's only desert, characterized by arid plains, rocky slopes, and unique geological formations. Additionally, the region is bordered by the Sierra de Los Filabres and Sierra de Alhamilla mountain ranges, offering challenging climbs and panoramic views. Many routes combine desert scenery with ascents into the surrounding hills.

Q: Are there many challenging road cycling routes in Tabernas?
A: Yes, Tabernas is well-known for its challenging road cycling routes. Out of over 20 routes available, 18 are rated as difficult, featuring significant elevation gains. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20119668" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Climb to Alto de Velefique – Velefique climb loop from Complejo Oro Verde&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 130 km with more than 3000 meters of elevation, are popular among advanced cyclists and have even been featured in professional races.

Q: Are there any easier or moderate road cycling routes suitable for less experienced riders?
A: While many routes are challenging, there are options for moderate difficulty. Currently, there are 3 routes rated as moderate. These routes still offer the unique Tabernas landscape but with less demanding climbs, making them suitable for those looking for a less strenuous ride while still enjoying the region's beauty.

Q: What kind of unique sights can I see along the road cycling routes in Tabernas?
A: Tabernas offers a blend of natural wonders and cinematic history. You can cycle through the dramatic desert landscapes, reminiscent of 'Spaghetti Westerns'. Highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/635124" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tabernas Oasis&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6419315" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tabernas Western Village Andalusia&lt;/a&gt;, which are old film sets. You can also encounter various mountain passes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1105863" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Climb to Alto de Velefique&lt;/a&gt;, offering stunning views.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Tabernas?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes in Tabernas are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular example is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984694445/the-spaghetti-western-of-spain-circular-desde-tabernas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Spaghetti Western of Spain — circular desde Tabernas&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a unique desert cycling experience, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e987137762/climb-to-colativi-from-tabernas-circular-route-through-the-sierra-de-alhamilla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Climb to Colativi from Tabernas — circular route through the Sierra de Alhamilla&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Tabernas?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 10 reviews. Cyclists often praise the unique desert scenery, the challenging mountain climbs, and the generally good quality, often traffic-free roads. The diverse badlands and varied topography also contribute to a memorable cycling experience.

Q: Are there any specific attractions like Mini Hollywood or Fort Bravo accessible by bike?
A: Yes, the Tabernas Desert is famous for its 'Spaghetti Western' film sets. While direct cycling access into the theme parks might be restricted, routes often pass by or near these iconic locations. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6419315" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tabernas Western Village Andalusia&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4599892" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Texas Hollywood&lt;/a&gt; are notable highlights in the area, allowing you to combine your ride with a cultural experience.

Q: Is there parking available for cyclists in Tabernas?
A: Yes, Tabernas village and other starting points for routes typically offer parking facilities. Given its growing popularity as a cycling destination, you'll find suitable places to park your vehicle before embarking on your ride. It's always a good idea to check specific starting points for detailed parking information.

Q: Can I find cafes or accommodation suitable for cyclists in Tabernas?
A: Tabernas and the surrounding villages cater to visitors, including cyclists. You can find local cafes for refreshments and meals, as well as various accommodation options ranging from hotels to guesthouses. These establishments are often accustomed to hosting outdoor enthusiasts.

Q: Are there any permits required for cycling in the Tabernas Desert or natural parks?
A: Generally, no specific permits are required for road cycling on public roads in the Tabernas Desert or through the Sierra de Alhamilla Natural Park. However, it's always advisable to respect local regulations, stay on marked routes, and be mindful of the fragile desert environment.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling options in Tabernas?
A: While many routes in Tabernas are geared towards experienced cyclists due to significant elevation, families with older children or those seeking less challenging rides can find suitable sections on quieter roads or explore the more moderate routes available. It's recommended to check the difficulty and elevation profiles of specific routes to ensure they match your family's ability.
tips: Oasys MiniHollywood is a small theme park that became famous in the last century due to the so-called "Spaghetti western", where the small town that resembles the Wild West is located a little further north. The theme park looks like the "set" where a large number of Wild West films were made.

From the road you can see what is called the Desierto de Tabernas, a set of valleys that look more like a desert than an area found in Spain. You can see palm trees, sand streams and mountains that resemble an oasis: a few kilometers from the Mediterranean Sea.
Olula de Castro is a town located at the foot of the Sierra de los Filabres, mountains with low vegetation but where the town is distinguished by the white walls of its houses.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the Spaghetti Western of Spain — circular desde Tabernas route?
faqs.answer: This circular route begins and ends in Tabernas. You can typically find parking within the town itself, which serves as a convenient starting point for your ride.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This is a challenging road cycling route designed for racebikes, covering a significant distance in a desert environment. It is generally not suitable or recommended for dogs due to the terrain, distance, and potential for extreme temperatures.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Tabernas Desert experiences extreme temperatures. The best times to cycle this route are during the cooler months of spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). Avoid the peak summer months when temperatures can soar, and be prepared for potential cold snaps in winter.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay an entrance fee to cycle in the Tabernas Desert?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need a special permit or to pay an entrance fee to cycle this route through the Paraje Natural del Desierto de Tabernas. However, as it is a protected natural area, please ensure you respect the environment by staying on designated paths and not disturbing wildlife.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route traverses the unique and arid landscapes of the Tabernas Desert, characterized by stark, ochre canyons, rocky slopes, and vast arid plains. While much of the route features mostly well-paved surfaces, its 'difficult' rating suggests that some segments might be unpaved or more challenging, requiring good bike handling skills.

faqs.question: What are the main highlights or viewpoints along the way?
faqs.answer: This route offers breathtaking panoramic vistas of the desert landscape. While cycling, you'll experience the iconic 'Spaghetti Western' scenery. Notable highlights in the area include Oasys MiniHollywood (Spanish Far West), Views of the Lawrence of Arabia Oasis, and the settlement of Olula de Castro.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginner road cyclists?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as a 'difficult' road ride and requires 'very good fitness'. With a distance of 64.4 kilometers and an elevation gain of 1,300 meters, it is best suited for experienced cyclists looking for a challenging ride in a unique environment.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other trails or paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, while primarily a road cycling route, it does intersect or briefly follow sections of other paths. You may encounter parts of trails such as CIMA AM08 Calar Alto * Gérgal, Sendero de las Cristaleras, Rambla de Castro, Rambla de la Galera, and Rambla Ancha.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a road cycling trip in the Tabernas Desert?
faqs.answer: Given the challenging nature and desert environment, it's crucial to bring plenty of water, sun protection (sunscreen, hat, sunglasses), appropriate cycling gear, repair kit, and snacks. Due to the extreme temperatures, layers are advisable depending on the season, and always check the weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to refill water along the route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily traverses an arid desert landscape. While it passes through Olula de Castro, services can be sparse. It's highly recommended to carry sufficient water and snacks for the entire duration of the ride, as reliable refill points may not be readily available.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular route?
faqs.answer: For a fit cyclist, this 64.4-kilometer route with 1,300 meters of elevation gain typically takes around 3 hours and 42 minutes to complete. However, this duration can vary significantly based on your fitness level, pace, and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

waypoints.name: Views of the Lawrence of Arabia Oasis
